steps.title: Use the intercept and slope as two points
steps.content: The $y$-intercept is $(0,-4)$. The slope is $3$, so moving right $1$ raises the output by $3$, giving a second point $(1,-1)$.

steps.title: Find the line from the data
steps.content: Slope-intercept form is $f(x)=mx+b$. The slope is $m=3$, and the $y$-intercept is $b=-4$, so $$f(x)=3x-4.$$

steps.title: Confirm the answer
steps.content: The correct answer is the only equation with slope $3$ and $y$-intercept $-4$. Therefore D is correct.
